title: What to Know About This Fund’s $14 Million Patrick Industries Exit After a Tough Quarter
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: Anchor Capital Management fully exited its position in Patrick Industries during Q1 2026, selling 116,967 shares worth approximately $14.46 million. The exit reduced Patrick Industries from 11.3% of the fund's assets to zero. Patrick Industries faces headwinds from softening RV and housing demand, though the company showed strength in marine and powersports segments with revenue of $997 million and maintained solid free cash flow of $194 million.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The fund's complete exit of an 11.3% position signals loss of confidence. Stock has plunged nearly 40% since February and underperformed S&P 500 by 17 percentage points. While the company maintains operational strength and free cash flow, macro pressures in RV and housing markets are weighing on investor sentiment.
Mentioned as a top holding in Anchor Capital's portfolio ($21.10 million, 22.8% of AUM) with no specific performance commentary provided in the article.
Listed as a top holding in Anchor Capital's portfolio ($20.43 million, 22.0% of AUM) with no specific performance commentary provided in the article.
Mentioned as a top holding in Anchor Capital's portfolio ($16.99 million, 18.3% of AUM) with no specific performance commentary provided in the article.

title: Patrick Industries, Inc. Declares Quarterly Cash Dividend
author: Prnewswire
description: Patrick Industries (NASDAQ:PATK) announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.47 per share, payable on June 8, 2026 to shareholders of record as of May 26, 2026. The dividend declaration was approved by the company's Board of Directors on May 14, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company's declaration of a quarterly cash dividend demonstrates financial strength and confidence in future cash flows. Regular dividend payments are a positive signal to investors, indicating the company's ability to generate profits while returning capital to shareholders.

title: Patrick Industries, Inc. to Participate in Upcoming CJS Conference
author: Prnewswire
description: Patrick Industries (NASDAQ:PATK), a leading component solutions provider for the RV, Marine, Powersports, and Housing markets, announced it will participate in CJS Securities' 26th Annual 'New Ideas for the New Year' Conference on January 14, 2026. The company's management team will present and hold one-on-one meetings with institutional investors and analysts at the virtual event.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The announcement is a routine corporate event notification regarding conference participation. While conference presentations can provide visibility to investors, the news itself is procedural in nature with no material business developments, financial results, or strategic announcements that would warrant a positive or negative sentiment.

title: Patrick Industries, Inc. to Participate in Upcoming Raymond James Conference
author: Prnewswire
description: Patrick Industries, a component solutions provider for Outdoor Enthusiast and Housing markets, will participate in the Raymond James 2025 TMT and Consumer Conference in New York from December 8-10, 2025. The company's management team will engage in a fireside chat and one-on-one meetings with institutional investors and analysts.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company is proactively engaging with investors, showcasing confidence by participating in a prominent conference and offering direct access to management, which suggests transparency and potential growth opportunities

title: Patrick Industries, Inc. Announces Increase to Regular Quarterly Cash Dividend
author: Prnewswire
description: Patrick Industries announced a dividend increase from $0.40 to $0.47 per share, payable on December 15, 2025, reflecting confidence in the company's business model and cash flow generation.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Company increased quarterly dividend, demonstrating financial strength, confidence in business performance, and commitment to shareholder value
